1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2â†’
 3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
 4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
 5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule
 7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
 8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
13â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
14â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
15â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
16â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
17â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
18â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
19â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
20â†’
21â†’/**
22â†’ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23â†’ */
24â†’public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25â†’    StandardRule(
26â†’        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28â†’            setOf(
29â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31â†’            ),
32â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33â†’    ),
34â†’    Rule.Experimental,
35â†’    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37â†’        node: ASTNode,
38â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40â†’    ) {
41â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42â†’            val afterBlockComment =
43â†’                node
44â†’                    .leaves()
45â†’                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46â†’                    .firstOrNull()
47â†’                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48â†’
49â†’            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50â†’                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51â†’                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52â†’            ) {
53â†’                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
55â†’                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56â†’                }
57â†’            }
58â†’        }
59â†’    }
60â†’
61â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65â†’        rawRemove()
66â†’    }
67â†’
68â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69â†’
70â†’    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72â†’        this
73â†’            ?.text
74â†’            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75â†’            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76â†’            ?.trim()
77â†’            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78â†’            ?: false
79â†’}
80â†’
81â†’public val NO_SINGLE_LINE_BLOCK_COMMENT_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule().ruleId
82â†’

275â†’                lintError(4, 5, "$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier"),
276â†’            )
277â†’        }
278â†’
279â†’        @Test
280â†’        fun `Given that a NoFooIdentifierRule violation is suppressed with custom formatter tags in block comments then do not find a violation in that block`() {
281â†’            val code =
282â†’                """
283â†’                /* custom-formatter-tag-off */
284â†’                val fooNotReported = "foo"
285â†’                /* custom-formatter-tag-on */
286â†’                val fooReported = "foo"
287â†’                """.trimIndent()
288â†’
289â†’            val actual =
290â†’                lint(
291â†’                    code,
292â†’                    editorConfigOverride =
293â†’                        EditorConfigOverride.from(
294â†’                            FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY to true,
295â†’                            FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY to "custom-formatter-tag-off",
296â†’                            FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY to "custom-formatter-tag-on",
297â†’                        ),
298â†’                )
299â†’            assertThat(actual).containsExactly(
300â†’                lintError(4, 5, "standard:no-foo-identifier-standard"),
301â†’                lintError(4, 5, "$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier"),
302â†’            )
303â†’        }
304â†’
305â†’        @Test
306â†’        fun `Given that a NoFooIdentifierRule violation is suppressed with custom formatter tags in EOL comments then do not find a violation in that block`() {
307â†’            val code =
308â†’                """
309â†’                // custom-formatter-tag-off
310â†’                val fooNotReported = "foo"
311â†’                // custom-formatter-tag-on
312â†’                val fooReported = "foo"
313â†’                """.trimIndent()
314â†’
315â†’            val actual =
316â†’                lint(
317â†’                    code,
318â†’                    editorConfigOverride =
319â†’                        EditorConfigOverride.from(
320â†’                            FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY to true,
321â†’                            FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY to "custom-formatter-tag-off",
322â†’                            FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY to "custom-formatter-tag-on",
323â†’                        ),
324â†’                )
325â†’            assertThat(actual).containsExactly(
326â†’                lintError(4, 5, "standard:no-foo-identifier-standard"),
327â†’                lintError(4, 5, "$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier"),
328â†’            )
329â†’        }
330â†’    }
331â†’
332â†’    private class NoFooIdentifierRule(id: RuleId) : Rule(
333â†’        ruleId = id,
334â†’        about = About(),
335â†’    ) {
336â†’        override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
337â†’            node: ASTNode,
338â†’            autoCorrect: Boolean,
339â†’            emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
340â†’        ) {
341â†’            if (node.elementType == ElementType.IDENTIFIER && node.text.startsWith("foo")) {
342â†’                emit(node.startOffset, "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
343â†’            }
344â†’        }
345â†’    }
346â†’
347â†’    private fun lint(
348â†’        code: String,
349â†’        editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
350â†’    ) = ArrayList<LintError>().apply {
351â†’        KtLintRuleEngine(
352â†’            ruleProviders =
353â†’                setOf(
354â†’                    // The same rule is supplied once a standard rule and once as non-standard rule. Note that the
355â†’                    // ruleIds are different.
356â†’                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
357â†’                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
358â†’                ),
359â†’            editorConfigOverride =
360â†’                editorConfigOverride
361â†’                    .plus(
362â†’                        STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
363â†’                        NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
364â†’                    ),
365â†’        ).lint(Code.fromSnippet(code)) { e -> add(e) }
366â†’    }
367â†’
368â†’    private fun lintError(
369â†’        line: Int,
370â†’        col: Int,
371â†’        ruleId: String,
372â†’    ) = LintError(line, col, RuleId(ruleId), "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)

378â†’        val NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier")

### Fixed", "new_string": "* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))

### Removed

* Drop support for `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives. Use `@Suppress` annotations instead. A new experimental rule `discouraged-comment-directive` is added to detect and report usage of the deprecated directives.

### Fixed"}

        fun `Issue 631 - Given some code for which indentation is disabled with ktlint-disable-enable-block then do not fix indentation of that block only`() {
            val code =
                """
                val fooWithIndentationFixing1: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                // ktlint-disable indent
                val fooWithIndentationFixingSuppressed: String =
                    "foo" +
                    "bar"
                // ktlint-enable indent
                val fooWithIndentationFixing2: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }", "new_string": "        @Test
        fun `Issue 631 - Given some code with deprecated ktlint-disable-enable-block directive then fix indentation of all blocks as directive is no longer supported`() {
            val code =
                """
                val fooWithIndentationFixing1: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                // ktlint-disable indent
                val fooWithIndentationFixingSuppressed: String =
                    "foo" +
                    "bar"
                // ktlint-enable indent
                val fooWithIndentationFixing2: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                val fooWithIndentationFixing1: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                // ktlint-disable indent
                val fooWithIndentationFixingSuppressed: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                // ktlint-enable indent
                val fooWithIndentationFixing2: String =
                    "foo" +
                        "bar"
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(7, 5, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }"}
